sections.paragraph:
fut. יִשָּׂא, inf. absol. נָשֹׂוא Jer 10:5 Hos 1:6 const. נְשׂא Isa 1:14 שֹׂוא Psa 89:10 with suff. נָשְׂאִי Psal. 28:2 but far more frequently שְׂאֵת with pref. בִּשְׂאֵת Exo 27:7, and לָשֵׂאת (very often), with suff. שְׂאֵתִי, שְׂאֵתֹו; imp. נְשָׂא (once נְסה Psa 4:7), and שָׂא Gen 27:3 Num 3:40 part. pass. נָשׂוּא (once נָשׂוּי like quiescents לה֞ Psa 32:1.)

(1) to take up, to lift up. (With this correspond Eth. ነሥአ፡ to take, to receive; see No. 3, and Arab. نشأ to be exalted, to grow, to increase; but other roots are used to express the idea of raising up, bearing, taking, as رفع, حمل.) Gen 7:17, “the waters increased and bore up the ark.” Gen 29:1, “and Jacob lifted up his feet and went,” etc. נָשָׂא נֵס to lift up, i.e. to set up a banner, Jer 4:6 50:2 51:12 , 27 Jer 51:27. Followed by עַל to place upon any one, Gen 31:17 נָשָׂא עָלָיו חֵטְא to lay sin upon oneself (i.e. to contract it), Lev 22:9 followed by בְּ 2Ch 6:22. Intrans. to lift up oneself, Psa 89:10 Nah 1:5.-Specially the following expressions should be remarked-(a) נָשָׂא יָד (and נָשָׂא כַף) to lift up the hand, in swearing, Deu 32:40 (compare הֵרִים יָד Gen 14:22 Dan 12:7 and Virg. Æn. xii. 195); hence to swear, followed by a dative of pers. and the gerund of a verb, Eze 20:6, נָשָׂאתִי יָדִי לָהֶם לְהֹוצִיאָם. Eze 47:14 Exo 6:8 Num 14:30 Psa 106:26 Neh 9:15. In other places it is for the purpose of doing violence, 2Sa 20:21 (followed by בְּ), to punish, Psa 10:12 to pray and adore, Psa 28:2 63:5 134:2 (compare Lam 3:41); as beckoning, Isa 13:2 49:22 .

(b) נָשָׂא רֹאשֹׁו to lift up one’s head-(α) used of one who is cheerful and merry, Job 10:15 Zec 2:4.-(β) of one who increases in wealth, becomes mighty (compare the Lat. caput extollere in civitate), Jud. 8:28 Psal. 83:3 but-(γ) נָשָׂא רֹאשׁ פּ׳ מִבֵּית כֶּלֶא to lift up the head of any one from prison, i.q. to cause him to go up out of a prison (which used to be under ground), to bring out of it, 2Ki 25:27 and without the words מִבֵּ׳ כּ׳ Gen 40:13, 20 Gen 40:20. For another meaning of this phrase, see No. 2, letter d.

(c) נָשָׂא פָנָיו to lift up one’s own countenance, used of a person of conscious rectitude, cheerful and full of confidence, Job 11:15. Ellipt. Gen 4:7, “if thou hast done well שְׂאֵת (there will be) lifting up (sc. of countenance);” i.e. thou mayest go with a cheerful countenance. Opp. to הִפִּיל פָּנָיו ver. 5, 6. Followed by אֶל to look upon, towards any thing, 2Ki 9:32 metaph. to place confidence in any person or thing, Job 22:26 2Sa 2:22 also used of God as beholding men with kindness, Num 6:26.

(d) נָשָׂא עֵינַיִם to lift up the eyes, with the verbosity which in such cases is used in Hebrew (compare, to lift up the feet, Gen 29:1 to lift up the voice, letter e, and under the verb לָקַח), often put before verbs of beholding, or seeing, as Gen 13:10, “he lifted up his eyes and saw;” Gen 13:14 18:2 31:10 33:1 , Gen 33:5 43:29 followed by אֶל and לְ to lift up the eyes on, to cast the eyes upon any person or thing; i.e. in love or desire, Gen 39:7 Psa 121:1 figuratively used of longing towards God, and confidence in him, Psa 123:1 in an idol, Eze 28:12 23:27 Deu 4:19. Compare letter g.

(e) נָשָׂא קֹול to lift up the voice, pleonast. (comp. letter d) before verbs of weeping, bewailing, Gen 27:38 29:11 Jud. 2:4 1Sa 24:17 30:4 of crying out, Jud. 9:7 of rejoicing, Isa 24:14 also without the word קֹול (Germ. anheben). Num 14:1, וַתִּשָּׂא כָל־הָעֵדָה … וַיִּבְכּוּ “the whole assembly lifted up (their voice) … and wept.” Isa 3:7, and even absol. in the sense of crying out, and rejoicing. Job 21:12, “they lift up the voice (i.e. they rejoice, they sing) to the sound of the timbrel and harp;” Isa 42:11. Isa 42:2, לֹא יִשָּׂא “he shall not lift up” the voice, i.e. he shall not cry with a loud voice, i.q. לֹא יִצְעַק. Compare מַשָּׂא No. 5. Hence-

(f) to lift up, to take up any thing with the voice, as a song, Num 23:7 Job 27:1 prayers, Isa 37:4 reproaches, Psa 15:3 the name of God, Exo 20:7 a false report, Exo 23:1.

(g) נָשָׂא נֶפֶשׁ אֶל to lift up the soul to anything, i.e. to wish for, to desire something (Arab. ellipt. حملالىشى), Deu 24:15 Hos 4:8 Pro 19:18 followed by לְ Psa 24:4 especially אֶל יְהֹוָה i.e. anxiously to long for the aid of God, Psa 25:1 86:4 143:8 .

(h) לִבִּי נְשָׂאַתְנִי “my heart has lifted me up,”-(α) i.e. it incites me to something, i.e. I am ready and prepared to do something, Exo 35:21, 26 Exo 35:26 36:2 . The same expression is used-(β) of pride. 2Ki 14:10, נְשָׂאֲךָ לִבֶּךָ “thy heart has lifted thee up,” i.e. thou liftest up thyself, thou art proud.

(i) to lift up in a balance, i.e. to weigh, Job 6:2. Comp. Lat. pendo and Heb. סָלָא.

(2) to bear, to carry (very frequently found), as an infant in the arms, Isa 46:4 garments, 1Sa 2:28 14:3 fruit, as a tree, Eze 17:8 Joe 2:22 Job 40:20 produce, as a field, Psa 72:3, etc. Gen 13:6, נָשָׂא אֹתָם הָאָרֶץ לָשֶׁבֶת יַחְדָּו לֹא “and the land did not bear (i.e. contain) them, that they might live together;” (but compare Job 21:3). Specially-(a) i.q. to endure, Isa 1:14 Mic 7:9 Pro 30:21 Psa 55:13. Hence to suffer, to bear with. Job 21:3, שָׂאוּנִי וְאָנֹכִי אֲדַבֵּר “bear with me, and I will speak.” Followed by בְּ partitive, to bear a part of any thing. Job 7:13, יִשָּׂא בְשִׂיחִי מִשְׁכָּבִי “my couch will bear part of my grief” (mein Bette wird mir meinen Kummer ertragen helfen, wird mit daran tragen).

(b) נָשָׂה עֲֹון פּ׳ to bear any one’s sin, i.e. to receive the punishment of sin upon oneself, Isa 53:12. בַּעֲֹון פּ׳ id., Eze 18:19, 20 Eze 18:20. חֶטְאֹו, נָשָׂא עֲֹונֹו to bear the punishment of one’s own sin, Levit. 5:1 , 17 17:16 20:19 24:15 Numb. 5:31 9:13 14:34 30:16 and so נָשָׂא זְנוּתָיו to bear the penalty of one’s whoredom, Num 14:33 Eze 23:35. Absol. to bear punishment. Job 34:31, נָשָׂאתִי לֹא אֶחְבֹּל “I have borne (punishment), I will offend no more.” For another use of the phrase, see letter e.

(c) to bear to any one, to bring (bringen), Exo 10:13 1Ki 10:11 18:12 2Ki 2:16 4:19 , and-(d) to carry away (wegnehmen), Dan 1:16 Eze 29:19 Mic 2:2 2Sa 5:21 i.q. to take any one away from life, to destroy, Job 32:22. נָשָׂא רֹאשׁ מֵעַל פּ׳ to take away the head from any one, to deprive of his head, Gen 40:19, where allusion is made to a similar phrase, as to which see above No. 1 b, γ.

(e) נָשָׂא עָֹון פּ׳ to take away any one’s sin, i.e.-(α) to expiate, make atonement for sin (as a priest), Lev 10:17.-(β) to pardon sin, Psa 32:5 85:3 Job 7:21 Gen 50:17. Followed by a dat. of pers., Gen 18:24, 26 Gen 18:26 Num 14:19 Isa 2:9 Hos 1:6. Part. pass. נְשֻׂא עָֹון whose sin is forgiven, Isa 33:24 and נְשׂוּי פֶּשַׁע id., Psa 32:1. From the sense of taking away comes-

(3) to take, to receive, to take hold of, Gen 27:3 “take now thy weapons … and go.” Gen 45:19, “take your father, and come;” (comp. Gr. λαβών, and the observations on לָקַח). Specially-

(a) נָשָׂא אִשָּׁה to take, i.e. to marry, a wife, in the later writers for לָקַח אִשָּׁה 2Ch 11:21 13:21 Ezr 10:44 hence ellipt. Ezr 9:2, “they took (wives) of their daughters for themselves and for their sons.” Ezr 9:12 Neh 13:25 2Ch 24:3.

(b) נָשָׂא פְּנֵי פ׳ to accept the person of any one, a phrase properly applicable to a king or judge, who receives those who come to salute him, and who bring gifts, and favours their cause (see especially Job 13:10). Opp. to הֵשִׁיב פָּנִים not to accept persons. Hence-(α) in a good sense, to receive any one’s prayer, to be favourable to it, to have respect to him as a petitioner, Gen 19:21 32:21 Job 42:8 Lam 4:16 Mal 1:8, Mal 1:9 Pro 6:35, לֹא־יִשָּׂא פְּנֵי כָל־כֹּפֶר “he regardeth no ransom.” Part. pass. נְשׂוּא פָנִים a man who is respected, who has much authority (pr. to whom no one denies a request or entreaty), 2Ki 5:1 Job 22:8 Isa 3:3 9:14 .-(β) in a bad sense, to be partial (spoken of a judge), Levit. 19:15 Deu 10:17 Psa 82:2 Job 13:8, 10 Job 13:10 32:21 34:19 Pro 18:5. Mal 2:9, נֹשָׂאִים פָּנִים בַּתֹּורָה “partial in (enforcing) the law,” compare הִכִּיר פְּנִים, and in N.T. πρόσωπον λαμβάνειν.

(c) נָשָׂא רֹאשׁ to take the sum (die Summe aufnehmen), the number of anything, to number, Exo 30:12 Num 1:2, 49 נָשָׂא מִסְפָּר id. Num 3:40.

Niphal, נִשָּׂא-

(1) to lift up oneself, to be elevated, -Eze. 1:1921 ; Psa 94:2 Isa 40:4 52:13 . Part. נִשָּׂא lifted up, high, Isa 2:2, 12 6:1 30:25 57:7 .

(2) to be carried, Exo 25:28 Isa 49:22 to be carried away, 2Ki 20:17.

Piel, נִשֵּׂא and נִשָּׂא-

(1) to lift up, to exalt, 2Sa 5:12 נִשֵּׂא נֶפֶשׁ לְ i.q. Kal No. 1, let. g; to desire anything greatly, Jer 22:27 44:14 .

(2) to help, to aid, Est 9:3 Psa 28:9 especially by gifts, 1Ki 9:11 Ezr 1:4 8:36 . Hence-

(3) to offer gifts; followed by לְ 2Sa 19:43.

(4) to take away, Amo 4:2.

Hiphil, הִשִּׂיא-

(1) causat. of Kal No. 2, b, to cause some one to bear guilt, i.e. to bear the punishment of his sin, Lev 22:16.

(2) followed by אֶל to put upon, to apply something to any thing, e.g. a rope, 2Sa 17:13.

Hithpael, הִתְנַשֵּׂא and הִנַּשֵּׂא-

(1) to be lifted up, to be exalted; followed by לְ over anything, 1Ch 29:11.

(2) to lift up oneself, i.e.-(a) to stand up in strength, Num 16:3 23:24 24:7 1Ki 1:5 (b) to be proud, Eze 17:14 Pro 30:32 followed by עַל to exalt oneself above, Num 16:3 Eze 29:15.

Derivatives נָשִׂיא, נְשׂוּאָה, נִשֵּׂאת, מַשָּׂא, מַשָּׂאָה, מַשְּׂאֵת, שְׂאֵת שִׂיא.
